key_points: Public facilities are services provided by the government for the benefit of all people
Examples of public facilities include water supply, electricity, public transport, schools and hospitals
These facilities are necessary for people's daily life and well-being
The government is responsible for ensuring that public facilities are available to everyone
Public facilities should be accessible equally to all citizens
Proper management of public facilities improves quality of life
examples: Clean drinking water supplied to homes by municipal corporations
Government hospitals providing medical treatment to citizens
Public transport systems such as buses and trains
Government schools providing education to children
formulas: Public Facilities → Services + Government responsibility + Public benefit
Basic services → Water + Electricity + Healthcare + Education
Social development → Public facilities + Equal access
exam_tips: Remember the definition of public facilities
Write examples such as water supply, hospitals and schools
Explain why public facilities are important for society
Mention the role of the government in providing these facilities
